task_completion.description: Create the CDB list file named 'threat-intel-blocklist'
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal or text editor showing the creation or editing of a file named 'threat-intel-blocklist' in the 'etc/lists/' directory (typically /var/ossec/etc/lists/threat-intel-blocklist).

task_completion.description: Populate the CDB list with the required IP and category pairs
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of the 'threat-intel-blocklist' file must show exactly the 8 IP:category pairs specified in the task (e.g., '45.33.32.156:network_scanner', '185.220.101.34:tor_exit_node', etc.).

task_completion.description: Create a custom rule with ID 100100 and level 12
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Wazuh custom rules file (e.g., local_rules.xml) being edited, showing a rule tag with attributes id="100100" and level="12".

task_completion.description: Configure the rule to perform an address_match lookup on the srcip field against the CDB list
task_completion.visual_evidence: The rule contains a list lookup tag matching: <list field="srcip" lookup="address_match">etc/lists/threat-intel-blocklist</list>.

task_completion.description: Configure the rule description and groups
task_completion.visual_evidence: The rule contains a <description> tag that includes the text 'threat intelligence' and a <group> tag that includes both 'syslog' and 'threat_intelligence'.

task_completion.description: Restart the Wazuh manager to apply changes
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing a command to restart the Wazuh manager, such as 'systemctl restart wazuh-manager' or '/var/ossec/bin/wazuh-control restart'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent is interacting with the actual Wazuh server environment
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal prompt or file paths should indicate the agent is working within the Wazuh directory structure (e.g., /var/ossec/) rather than a generic local mock directory.

integrity.description: Verify the custom rule is written in valid XML format
integrity.visual_evidence: The rule configuration should be properly enclosed in XML tags (e.g., <group>, <rule>, <description>) and not just written as plain text.

integrity.description: Verify the agent didn't just echo text to bypass checks
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should use a text editor (like nano or vim) or proper append commands to edit the actual Wazuh configuration files, rather than just echoing the expected strings into arbitrary files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The CDB list must be named 'threat-intel-blocklist' and contain exactly these 8 entries: 45.33.32.156:network_scanner, 185.220.101.34:tor_exit_node, 91.189.114.8:brute_force_attacker, 104.248.36.228:malware_distributor, 45.148.10.78:botnet_c2, 185.56.80.65:apt_infrastructure, 23.129.64.130:tor_exit_node, 171.25.193.20:tor_exit_node. The custom rule must have ID 100100, level 12, include 'threat intelligence' in its description, and include 'threat_intelligence' and 'syslog' in its groups. The rule must use <list field="srcip" lookup="address_match">etc/lists/threat-intel-blocklist</list>.
